metadata.notes: agent=yes daemon=bug driver=ready — FROZEN (not recordable). agy never binds a PID to its conv-id transcript session (pid=0, transcript-first), so during a long idle there is no process-liveness signal and the readyTTL sweep reaps the session — the same documented caveat as the IDE surface. Long-idle SURVIVAL is therefore unobservable for antigravity until the optional PID bind (real cwd via history.jsonl) lands; tracked as a follow-up. Other lifecycle cells (1.1/1.2/1.4) are recorded.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

**agent=yes.** `agy`'s interactive REPL stays alive between turns and resumes on the next prompt — verified live (a single agy process accepted a second prompt and appended a fresh turn to the same transcript).

**daemon=full.** The ExactName{agy} matcher (`adapter.go:36`) keeps the session bound to its live PID, so the PID-liveness check holds the session in ready past readyTTL rather than sweeping it on idle. The next user prompt re-opens the turn (USER_INPUT → user_message → working, `parser.go:63`) and the terminal PLANNER_RESPONSE settles it back to ready. Single native process per conversation makes the liveness check unambiguous.

**driver=gap:wait_turn.** The recipe is two send+wait_turn turns around a long sleep; `wait_turn` is a not_implemented stub in the scaffold driver. `record` ports the turn-done poll before driving.## Reasoning

agent=yes daemon=bug driver=ready — FROZEN (not recordable). agy never binds a PID to its conv-id transcript session (pid=0, transcript-first), so during a long idle there is no process-liveness signal and the readyTTL sweep reaps the session — the same documented caveat as the IDE surface. Long-idle SURVIVAL is therefore unobservable for antigravity until the optional PID bind (real cwd via history.jsonl) lands; tracked as a follow-up. Other lifecycle cells (1.1/1.2/1.4) are recorded.
caveats: Antigravity emits no explicit end-of-turn marker; the daemon infers turn_done from a terminal MODEL/PLANNER_RESPONSE with no tool_calls. The no-tool prompts make this inference unambiguous, but the ready-settle is provisional on emission until a live recording confirms it.

Recording uses IRRLICHT_READY_SESSION_TTL=15s so a ~20s idle window cleanly exceeds the TTL; the spec asserts duration_at_least (a floor), so production multi-hour idles are by definition still passing.
observations omit cost/tokens — antigravity's JSONL carries no usage.

sources.note: ExactName{agy} keeps the session bound to its live PID (liveness past TTL)
USER_INPUT re-opens the turn → working on the next prompt

preconditions: agy CLI on PATH (1.0.10), Google OAuth authenticated; no API key
irrlichd running with IRRLICHT_READY_SESSION_TTL=15s (default 30 min would force a 32+ min recording)
irrlichd in --record mode with grant-all permission mode (transcripts permission)
DRIVER GAP (wait_turn): not_implemented in driver-interactive.sh — record ports the turn-done poll first
setup: Driver launches agy --dangerously-skip-permissions in a fresh cwd; the daemon binds the agy PID via ExactName + cwd.
The post-turn sleep (20s) is ~1.33x a 15s readyTTL — long enough to prove 'past TTL'.

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok
Reply with exactly the word: alive

verify: events.jsonl shows the agy session entering ready after turn 1
no transcript_removed for the session during the 20s idle window (not swept while agy is alive)
no state_transition to working/waiting during the idle window (state stays ready)
after the second send: ready → working → ready for the same session
